input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A white plate displays dried dark purple flowers, a thin orange root, a large yellow wedge, and a small pile of green dried leaves. Additional brown roots appear on the right edge. A colorful package and woven mat are visible in the background. The camera remains static on these ingredients.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

Additional brown roots appear on the right edge, with a colorful package and woven mat in the background. The camera remains static on these ingredients. [10-12] The camera zooms in closer on the white plate, cropping out the colorful background package. The view pans slightly right, bringing the bundle of brown roots on the far edge into clearer focus. The hand withdraws from the frame. [12-14] The camera tilts upward and left, causing the yellow wedge and orange root to exit the frame, centering on dried dark purple flowers and green leaves on a white plate. [14-16] A hand enters from the upper left to grasp a purple flower as the camera tilts down, returning the yellow wedge and orange root to view. [16-18] The camera zooms in and pans right, emphasizing the bundle of brown roots on the right edge

In the background, a small white artificial Christmas tree with fluffy branches is decorated with yellow and white spherical ornaments and gold bead garlands. queries.question: What entities are present on the white plate and how are they arranged?

answers.text: The white plate features dried dark purple flowers positioned in the upper section, with a large yellow lemon wedge at the bottom center. A brown root lies on the left side, while green tea leaves occupy the right. This arrangement rests on a woven mat alongside a colorful gift box in the background. The items remain mostly stationary throughout the sequence, clearly displaying the distinct separation of the floral, citrus, root, and leaf components on the ceramic surface.
